question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a request with conflicting but technically valid filter combinations (e.g., spender.id=gte:X&spender.id=lte:X for a single spender) to extractTokenMultiUnionQuery(), causing the query to behave as an equality check while appearing as a range query, potentially bypassing any future rate limiting or access controls that might be applied differently to equality vs range queries?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the unauthenticated token allowance endpoint to perform reconnaissance before a token theft attack, identifying which tokens a victim has approved for spending, the exact amounts approved, and which spender accounts control those approvals, providing a complete attack surface map without any authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of authentication in getAccountTokenAllowances() to monitor a victim account's token allowances in real-time, detecting when new allowances are created (new spender/token combinations appear) and immediately attempting to exploit those allowances before the owner can revoke them?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the token allowance endpoint to identify accounts that have granted unlimited or very large token allowances (high amount_granted values) without authentication, targeting those accounts for social engineering attacks to compromise the spender accounts that control those large allowances?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the token allowance endpoint returns the spender field in shard.realm.num format via EntityId.parse() in BaseAllowanceViewModel, allowing the attacker to enumerate all spender account IDs for a victim owner without authentication and use those IDs to query the spenders' own allowances, building a multi-hop authorization graph?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the token allowance endpoint to determine whether a victim account has granted allowances to any accounts in a known list of compromised or malicious accounts (by querying with spender.id=eq:<known_bad_actor>), without authentication, enabling threat intelligence gathering about the victim's exposure to known bad actors?
